title: Zimbabwe cervical cancer biorepository: FFPE specimens with HIV documentation
institution: Parirenyatwa Group of Hospitals / University of Zimbabwe
access_route: Collaborative agreement with University of Zimbabwe College of Health Sciences; IRB and MTA required; contact corresponding author
notes: n=410 FFPE cervical cancer; HIV status documented (serology); squamous cell carcinoma and adenocarcinoma; FIGO stage I-II dominant; young patient population (median age <45); HPV type on subset

title: HIV-HPV co-infection and cervical cancer risk in Zimbabwe
institution: Parirenyatwa Group of Hospitals / University of Zimbabwe
access_route: Same institutional access as primary biorepository paper; collaborative research with UZ
notes: Draws from same Zimbabwe biorepository; HPV genotyping and HIV serology documented; confirms HIV screening approach; FIGO stage I-II in majority; young women (20-44) dominant

title: COEUR: a Canadian multisite ovarian cancer biobank
institution: COEUR / CRCHUM / Canadian Cancer Trials Group
access_route: Application to COEUR Scientific Committee; coordinated through CRCHUM Montreal; 12 contributing Canadian biobanks
notes: n=1246 HGSC (high-grade serous carcinoma); FFPE + serum matched; ER/PR annotation available on subset; FIGO stage III dominates; 12 biobanks contributing; longitudinal serum at surgery and recurrence

title: Washington University Gynecologic Oncology Tissue Bank: ovarian cancer specimens with matched plasma
institution: Washington University in St. Louis Gynecologic Oncology Tissue Bank
access_route: Collaborative research agreement with WashU GynOnc; IRB and MTA required
notes: n=227 ovarian cancer; FFPE + plasma matched; ER annotation documented; Stage III/IV dominant; HER2 status available on subset; detailed treatment history (platinum, taxane) recorded

title: Estrogen receptor expression in high-grade serous ovarian cancer: an HGSC TMAs study
access_route: TMA slides available through consortium agreement; contact corresponding author
notes: n=875 HGSC FFPE TMAs with ER/PR IHC scoring; no matched plasma; validates ER annotation approach; useful as annotation reference; FIGO stage III predominant

title: NRG Biospecimen Bank (GOG legacy): ovarian cancer FFPE, plasma, and serum archive
access_route: NRG Oncology correlative science committee application; nrgoncology.org/biospecimen-bank
notes: >3.5 million specimens from GOG legacy trials; ovarian cancer dominant; FFPE + plasma + serum from randomised trial participants; ER/PR annotation from trial protocols; access requires NRG membership or collaboration

title: BQC19: a Quebec COVID-19 biobank
access_route: BQC19 data/sample access application at bqc19.ca; ethics approval required
notes: n>6200 at publication; vaccinated subsets documented with vaccine type and dates; longitudinal plasma/serum; PBMC cryopreserved; multi-hospital Quebec consortium; COVID severity and outcomes annotated

title: PlasCoV: a large Canadian COVID-19 convalescent plasma biobank with vaccinated donor subsets
access_route: Contact BiobanqueCOVID@hema-quebec.qc.ca; application-based; 86,000+ samples available
notes: n=15,502 donors; 86,000+ individual samples; vaccinated donors with vaccine type documented; convalescent plasma focus but serum also available; direct contact email published in paper

title: LPCE Nice biobank: COVID-19 plasma, serum, and cellular biospecimens
institution: LPCE / Universite Cote d'Azur / CHU Nice
access_route: Collaboration with LPCE biobank, CHU Nice; IRB approval required; contact via corresponding author
notes: K2EDTA plasma, serum, and buffy coat at -80C; French single-center COVID biobank; vaccinated subsets documented from 2021 onward; detailed clinical severity annotation

title: CARI / Khon Kaen University cholangiocarcinoma biobank: world's largest CCA collection
journal: Asian Pacific Journal of Cancer Prevention
institution: Cholangiocarcinoma Research Institute (CARI) / Khon Kaen University
access_route: Collaborative research agreement with CARI, Khon Kaen University, Thailand; contact via PI Prof. Banchob Sripa or institute email
notes: World's largest CCA biobank; n=101 plasma + n=78 FFPE documented in substudy publications; T-stage annotation available; endemic region (liver fluke-associated CCA); MTA required for international collaboration

title: Mayo Clinic Hepatobiliary Registry: molecular characterisation of cholangiocarcinoma
access_route: Collaborative research through Mayo Clinic Hepatobiliary Research Program; IRB and MTA required
notes: ~80 CCA patients with matched FFPE + plasma; T2N1 staging documented; surgical resection specimens; western CCA population (non-fluke); pathology review confirmed; IHC panel available

title: Matched FFPE and plasma cfDNA for liquid biopsy in cholangiocarcinoma
access_route: Contact corresponding author; small pilot — institutional collaboration required
notes: Pilot study n=10 matched FFPE + plasma cfDNA; proof-of-concept for liquid biopsy; demonstrates cfDNA extraction feasibility from archived samples; not a biobank resource per se but demonstrates methodology

title: The French Glioblastoma Biobank (FGB): a nationwide infrastructure for GBM research
access_route: Application to FGB steering committee; coordinated by INSERM with 24 contributing French centers; contact fgb@inserm.fr
notes: n=1087 patients from 24 centers; fresh frozen + FFPE paired; plasma and serum available; IDH status, MGMT methylation, and EGFR amplification documented; age at diagnosis available — young patient subset identifiable; multisectorial sampling for ITH studies

title: Recurrent glioblastoma: molecular landscape in the French Glioblastoma Biobank
access_route: Application to FGB steering committee (same access route as primary biobank)
notes: Recurrent GBM subset of FGB; paired primary and recurrence specimens; plasma longitudinal; temozolomide treatment history documented; useful for treatment-failure cohort enrichment

title: Pilot metabolomics study of fresh/frozen GBM tissue and matched plasma
access_route: Contact corresponding author; FGB affiliate
notes: Pilot metabolomics study; fresh/frozen GBM + matched plasma; demonstrates metabolite extraction feasibility from frozen GBM; IDH1 wild-type confirmed; small n but validates pre-analytical protocol

title: Characterization of participants in the Severe Asthma Research Program (SARP III)
journal: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine
access_route: BioLINCC application (biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov); SARP III specimens available via NHLBI BioLINCC
notes: ICS, OCS, and biologic medication fully documented per subject; severe asthma focus; PBMC, serum, and plasma paired; multi-site US; n=714 with longitudinal visits

title: SARP I and II: clinical features of severe asthma
journal: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine
access_route: BioLINCC application (biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov)
notes: SARP I+II combined n=726; medication documented including ICS dose, OCS use, and controller regimen; sputum differential cell count available; serum IgE and eosinophil counts documented

title: BREATHE: a large-scale multi-centre cohort study of adult asthma in Denmark and Sweden
access_route: Application to BREATHE steering committee; contact via corresponding author at Copenhagen University Hospital
notes: n=1403 adults; ICS, LABA, OCS, and biologic therapy documented; serum and plasma paired; Scandinavian population; registry linkage with national prescription databases for medication verification

title: SPIROMICS: design and protocol for a multi-site study of COPD
journal: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine
access_route: BioLINCC application (biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov); SPIROMICS materials available via request to NHLBI
notes: n=2900 main cohort; ~250 underwent bronchoscopy with BAL collection; K2EDTA plasma + buffy at -80C; multi-site US; GOLD stage 0-4 documented; smoking history detailed

title: Characterization of the lower airway microbiota and inflammatory response in COPD: a SPIROMICS study
journal: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine
access_route: BioLINCC application (biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov)
notes: Blood and BAL matched from same visit; immunophenotyping by flow cytometry; microbiome profiling from BAL; SPIROMICS bronchoscopy sub-cohort

title: BAL metabolomics in COPD: a SPIROMICS substudy
journal: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine
access_route: BioLINCC application (biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov)
notes: Matched BAL + plasma from n=115 SPIROMICS participants; metabolomics assay performed; demonstrates BAL metabolite readout feasibility; GOLD stage and FEV1 documented

title: UAB Pulmonary Biorepository: design and initial results
institution: University of Alabama at Birmingham (UAB) Pulmonary Biorepository
access_route: Collaborative agreement with UAB Division of Pulmonary, Allergy and Critical Care Medicine; IRB required
notes: Single-center biorepository with BAL, plasma, and buffy coat matched per subject; COPD and asthma subjects; spirometry and imaging documented; contact via Dr. J. Edwin Blalock or successors

title: The Swedish Heart Failure Registry biobank: rationale, design, and initial findings
institution: Karolinska Institutet / Swedish Heart Failure Registry
access_route: Swedish HF Registry biobank committee application; linked to SwedeHF national registry
notes: Cardiac tissue paired with plasma and omics layers; comorbidity burden (including atherosclerosis) documented via national registry linkage; longitudinal follow-up with mortality endpoints

title: SWEDEHEART: a national quality registry and research platform for cardiovascular disease
access_route: SWEDEHEART data access committee; Uppsala Biobank for specimens; national registry linkage standard
notes: EDTA plasma at -80C; large national registry with atherosclerosis and CHF subsets; detailed medication and comorbidity annotation; specimen availability depends on subset enrollment

title: Endomyocardial biopsy-derived transcriptomic signatures identify subtypes of dilated cardiomyopathy
access_route: Collaborative research agreement with JHU cardiology; IRB approval required
notes: Matched endomyocardial biopsy + plasma from 88 HF patients; atherosclerosis comorbidity documented; RNA extraction from biopsy confirmed; small n limits statistical power but high tissue quality

title: Human heart biobanks: the cornerstone of basic and translational cardiac research
journal: Journal of Molecular and Cellular Cardiology
access_route: Sydney Heart Bank: sydneyheartbank.org.au; BMCB: collaborative agreement with UBC
notes: Review describing two major cardiac biobanks; Sydney Heart Bank has >2700 cardiac samples; BMCB (British Columbia Molecular Biomarker Consortium) plasma + tissue matched; both accessible via application

title: The Vejle Diabetes Biobank: a resource for studies of the aetiological heterogeneity of type 2 diabetes
institution: Vejle Hospital / Steno Diabetes Center Odense
access_route: Material Transfer Agreement with Vejle Hospital biobank; contact via corresponding author
notes: BMI, HbA1c, and lipid panel documented per subject; longitudinal design with up to 10-year follow-up; EDTA plasma collected fasting

title: The Danish Centre for Strategic Research in Type 2 Diabetes (DD2) cohort: characteristics of the first patients
access_route: Application to DD2 steering committee (www.dd2.nu); national Danish research biobank
notes: National incident T2D cohort; medication fully documented; BMI, eGFR, albuminuria captured at baseline; urine albumin-creatinine ratio available

title: Chronic Renal Insufficiency Cohort (CRIC) Study: design and methods
journal: Journal of the American Society of Nephrology
access_route: repository.niddk.nih.gov; application-based access with IRB and DUA required
notes: 47% of participants diabetic; annual biospecimen collection; urine and plasma available longitudinally; BMI documented at each visit; multi-site US cohort

title: Circulating Cell-Free DNA and Tumor Tissue Profiling in Metastatic Melanoma: A Comparative Study
access_route: corresponding author / institutional biobank request
notes: Treatment-naive metastatic melanoma cohort. Paired EDTA plasma and tumor tissue collected at baseline before systemic therapy. Multi-institutional collaboration between Semmelweis (Budapest) and Lund.

title: Establishment of a Melanoma Biospecimen Repository at NYU Langone Medical Center
institution: NYU Langone Medical Center / Interdisciplinary Melanoma Cooperative Group (IMCG)
access_route: IMCG biobank request / MTA
notes: 974 blood specimens (EDTA) and 1142 FFPE blocks from melanoma patients. Large established biobank with formal access route via NYU IMCG. Treatment-naive status not uniformly documented for all specimens.

title: The Lund Melanoma Biobank: A Resource for Translational Research
access_route: Lund University biobank / Cancer Moonshot collaboration request
notes: Lund Cancer Moonshot melanoma biobank. Tissue and blood stored at -80C. Formal biobank infrastructure with multi-institutional access potential. Exact N not specified in abstract.

title: epiMelanoma: Epigenomic Characterisation of Melanoma Using K2-EDTA Plasma cfDNA
access_route: consortium data access request / corresponding author
notes: 199 participants with K2-EDTA plasma for cfDNA epigenomics. Consortium study — access route requires contact with lead PI. Storage conditions not specified in available metadata.

title: Stage III Melanoma FFPE Biobank at the Netherlands Cancer Institute
access_route: NKI biobank / MTA required
notes: Stage III melanoma treatment-naive FFPE specimens at NKI Amsterdam. Well-documented biobank with formal MTA-based access. Blood/plasma not mentioned — tissue only.

title: Streck Cell-Free DNA BCT Tubes for cfDNA Preservation in Lung Squamous Cell Carcinoma
access_route: not_available — research use only, not banked for external access
notes: CONFIRMED NOT BANKED. Study used Streck BCT as collection method for a research protocol; specimens were processed and consumed for the study. No biobank infrastructure described. n=49 LUSC cases — not available for external sourcing.

title: Shallow Whole-Genome Sequencing of cfDNA from Streck BCT Tubes in Lung Cancer
access_route: not_available — research use only, not banked for external access
notes: CONFIRMED NOT BANKED. 16 SCC cases collected in Streck BCT for sWGS research. Specimens processed for the study, not retained in biobank. Small n, no access route described.

title: The National ALS Biorepository: A CDC-Funded Resource for ALS Research
journal: Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis and Frontotemporal Degeneration
access_route: CDC/ATSDR formal application; biorepository.cdc.gov
notes: 1721 enrolled participants with DNA, blood, serum, and CSF collected longitudinally. CDC-funded with formal public access mechanism. This is the largest and most accessible ALS biorepository in the US with the full trio (CSF+serum+DNA). Access requires CDC application process.

title: AMBRoSIA: A Multi-Site UK ALS Biomarker Study with Longitudinal CSF and Serum Collection
access_route: AMBRoSIA consortium data/sample access request via lead site (Oxford)
notes: 258 ALS cases + 80 controls, serum and CSF collected longitudinally. UK multi-site consortium with Oxford as lead. DNA not listed among collected specimen types — partial trio. Longitudinal design adds value for biomarker trajectory studies. Access via Oxford lead PI.

title: NEALS Sample Repository
access_route: neals.org sample access application; MGH-coordinated
notes: Non-paper source. NEALS Sample Repository offers CSF, serum, DNA, and PBMC — the full requested trio plus PBMC. Coordinated through MGH/Harvard. Application process at neals.org. N not publicly specified; inventory varies by specimen type. Strong access infrastructure for academic researchers.

title: Target ALS Biorepository
access_route: Target ALS data/sample access request; targetals.org
notes: 62 ALS cases + 51 controls across 18 US sites. CSF, plasma, and serum collected. DNA not explicitly listed as banked specimen type. Nonprofit foundation with formal access process. Multi-site collection increases generalizability. N is smaller than National Biorepository.

title: Paired CSF and Serum Proteomics in Multiple Sclerosis: A Welsh Neuroscience Biobank Study
journal: Annals of Clinical and Translational Neurology
institution: Cardiff University / Welsh Neuroscience Research Initiative (WNRI)
access_route: Welsh Neuroscience Research Initiative biobank / corresponding author
notes: 157 participants with paired CSF and serum. Recent collection (2024 publication). Welsh Neuroscience biobank infrastructure. Subtype breakdown (RRMS, SPMS, PPMS) not specified in abstract. Strong candidate for recent-collection criterion given 2024 publication date.

title: Matched CSF and Serum Biomarker Profiles in MS Subtypes at Dartmouth-Hitchcock
access_route: corresponding author / institutional biobank request
notes: 67 matched CSF and serum samples from MS patients. 2021 publication. Smaller cohort. Access via corresponding author. MS subtype breakdown likely available in full paper. Recent-collection criterion marginally met (2021).

title: CSF and Serum Multi-Omics in Progressive MS: A University of Nebraska Lincoln Study
access_route: corresponding author / institutional biobank request
notes: 206 progressive MS + 46 RRMS with CSF, serum, and urine. Recent collection (2024). Large n for an academic MS cohort. Progressive MS overrepresented — useful for progressive subtypes specifically. Access via corresponding author.

title: Serum and CSF Neurofilament Dynamics in MS: A Seville Hospital Longitudinal Study
access_route: corresponding author / Spanish biobank network (ISCIII)
notes: 40 MS patients with paired CSF and serum. 2023 publication. Small cohort. Spanish hospital with potential access via ISCIII biobank network. Longitudinal design adds value but small n limits usability as primary source.

title: Large-Scale MS Biobank at IRYCIS Madrid: 535 Participants with Paired CSF and Serum
access_route: IRYCIS biobank / ISCIII biobank network / corresponding author
notes: 535 MS participants with paired CSF and serum. Largest cohort in this bundle. 2025 publication — most recent collection. IRYCIS (Instituto Ramon y Cajal de Investigacion Sanitaria) has institutional biobank infrastructure. Access via ISCIII network or direct IRYCIS request. PMID not available at time of writing (PMC-only).

title: The Parkinson's Progression Markers Initiative (PPMI): A Decade of Progress
institution: PPMI / Michael J. Fox Foundation (50 sites worldwide)
access_route: ppmi-info.org data/sample access; Coriell biorepository for specimens
notes: 4200+ participants at 50 sites with serum, PBMC, DNA, CSF, and urine. Full trio (serum+PBMC+DNA) confirmed. Age distribution spans broad PD range — age >75 subset needs filtering from the full cohort; not a dedicated elderly-onset PD collection. Specimens distributed via Coriell Institute. Well-documented access process through PPMI portal.

title: The Parkinson's Disease Biomarkers Program (PDBP): A Resource for PD Research
access_route: NINDS PDBP data access; contact PDBP-OPS@mail.nih.gov; specimens via Coriell
notes: 1082 participants with CSF, DNA, PBMC, and serum. Full trio confirmed. NINDS-funded with formal access via Coriell Institute. Direct operations contact: PDBP-OPS@mail.nih.gov. Age >75 subset available but requires cohort filtering — not pre-stratified by age. Established access infrastructure with NIH backing.

title: Parkinson's Disease within the UK Biobank: Genetic and Phenotypic Characteristics
access_route: UK Biobank access application; ukbiobank.ac.uk
notes: PD cases within 500K participant UK Biobank. Serum and DNA available; PBMC not confirmed as banked specimen type for UK Biobank. Age >75 cases available given UK Biobank age range (40-69 at recruitment, now older with follow-up). PBMC gap is the key limitation for this bundle. Formal application-based access with processing fees.

title: The ProCaB biobank: a prospective collection of bladder and prostate cancer biospecimens at UZ Leuven

specimens_described.storage: FFPE archival, urine and blood at -80C

access_route: ProCaB biobank access request via UZ Leuven Biobank; Belgian ethics committee approval required
notes: Bladder cancer including T2+ MIBC with matched FFPE + urine + blood. Biobank explicitly described with named infrastructure (ProCaB). Prospective collection is a strong quality signal for pre-analytical consistency.

title: The UROSCAN cohort: a Swedish population-based bladder cancer biobank with long-term follow-up
access_route: UROSCAN consortium access request; Swedish ethical review board approval; population-based registry linkage
notes: Large n=1825 population-based bladder cancer biobank. FFPE + blood with long-term follow-up is a major scale signal. Urine not described in this cohort — partial match on specimen type. UROSCAN is well-indexed and access-route clear.

title: Multi-omic analysis of the urothelial carcinoma genome reveals oncogenic subtypes with therapeutic implications
institution: BCAN (Bladder Cancer Advocacy Network) / UC-GENOME consortium

specimens_described.storage: FFPE + plasma + PBMCs (storage conditions in methods)

access_route: BCAN data access request; UC-GENOME consortium data sharing agreement; dbGaP deposition possible
notes: n=218 metastatic urothelial carcinoma with FFPE + plasma + PBMCs. Multi-omic data available. Urine not described — partial match. DOI-only citation; verify PMID and PMC indexing for pipeline recall testing.

title: Lung Biobank Heidelberg: A Collection of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Biospecimens for Translational Research
institution: Heidelberg University Hospital / DZL (German Center for Lung Research)
access_route: Lung Biobank Heidelberg formal application process; DZL consortium governance; IRB approval required
notes: Stage IIIB/IV NSCLC with matched serum + FFPE at -80C. Biobank infrastructure explicitly described. DZL consortium membership may be required for access. Treatment-naive cohort clearly documented.

title: Profiling of cfDNA and circulating tumor DNA in treatment-naive advanced non-small cell lung cancer
access_route: Corresponding author contact; Chinese multicenter IRB approval; specimen availability not guaranteed
notes: Treatment-naive advanced NSCLC. Exact n not specified here — verify from abstract. Chinese multicenter cohort; cross-border specimen transfer may be restricted. Serum not described — FFPE + plasma cfDNA only, which partially matches the bundle's serum requirement.

title: Detection and Prognostic Value of Circulating Tumor DNA in Patients With Colorectal Cancer
access_route: Corresponding author at Aarhus University Hospital; Danish Data Protection Agency approval; institutional MTA required
notes: Stages I-III CRC with matched FFPE + preoperative plasma. JAMA Oncology publication = high indexing confidence. Danish multicenter = access may require coordination across sites. Treatment-naive preoperative collection is a strong quality signal for ADC biomarker work.

title: Circulating tumor DNA dynamics predict outcomes in stage III colon cancer treated with adjuvant chemotherapy
access_route: Corresponding author at Fudan University; Chinese ethics committee approval; cross-border specimen transfer subject to Chinese regulations
notes: n=165 stage III colon with FFPE + serial plasma. Adjuvant chemotherapy cohort — not treatment-naive. Serial plasma is a differentiating feature. Cross-border specimen transfer from China is a real access barrier; consider data-only collaboration as alternative.

title: ctDNA as a biomarker for adjuvant chemotherapy in colorectal cancer: a prospective multicenter study
access_route: Multi-country access: Danish PI contact + Spanish ethics approval; GDPR compliance required for EU-based cohort
notes: n=168 stage III CRC with FFPE + plasma. Danish+Spanish multicenter = broader geographic representation. PMC indexed = high recall probability. GDPR-compliant access pathway required for EU institutions.

title: A population-based study of PLCO colorectal cancer specimens: matched FFPE tumor and blood
institution: PLCO (Prostate, Lung, Colorectal, and Ovarian) Screening Trial / NCI
access_route: NCI Cancer Data Access System (CDAS) formal application; IRB approval; PLCO data sharing agreement
notes: PLCO NCI tissue resource with matched FFPE + blood. Large population-based screening trial. Plasma specifically not described — blood specimens may be serum/plasma/buffy coat; verify matrix in CDAS data dictionary. NCI CDAS access is structured but slower (3-6 months typical).

title: Neoadjuvant Pembrolizumab plus Chemotherapy in Early Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: ARTEMIS Trial
access_route: Corresponding author contact; MD Anderson tissue procurement core
notes: Treatment-naive TNBC, pre-NACT collection. Paired FFPE + blood makes this high-value for ADC biomarker development.

title: Genomic and transcriptomic characterization of triple-negative breast cancer reveals molecular subtypes and subtype-specific therapeutic vulnerabilities
access_route: BC Cancer biobank request via corresponding author; BC Cancer Research Ethics Board approval required
notes: Large TNBC cohort with matched FFPE + plasma cfDNA. Treatment naive status mixed — verify per patient metadata.

title: Serial liquid biopsies and tumor mutational evolution in metastatic triple-negative breast cancer
access_route: MD Anderson tissue procurement; corresponding author for plasma aliquots
notes: Smaller n=42 with serial plasma — valuable for longitudinal biomarker questions. FFPE archival; treatment history present in cohort.

title: Proteomics of paired pancreatic tissue and serum samples reveals biomarkers for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
institution: Lund University / CEBMMS (Center for Biobank, Epidemiology, and Biobanking)

specimens_described.storage: matched tissue + serum (n=29 pairs) + 53 additional serum; storage conditions in methods

access_route: Lund University Biobank (CEBMMS) formal application; Swedish regional ethics approval (Region Skane); MTA required
notes: n=29 matched PDAC tissue+serum pairs + 53 standalone serum. CEBMMS is a named biobank with established access process. Proteomics context = strong pre-analytical documentation expected. Treat n=29 as the matched pairs count, n=82 total serum-accessible.

title: The Barts Pancreas Tissue Bank: a repository of pancreatic cancer biospecimens with linked clinical data
institution: Barts Cancer Institute / Barts Pancreas Tissue Bank (BPTB)
access_route: BPTB formal access request via Barts Cancer Institute; NHS Research Ethics Committee approval; data sharing agreement; UK Biobank-style governance
notes: n=340 PDAC with the broadest specimen diversity in this bundle: FFPE + serum + plasma + urine + linked clinical data. BPTB is a named, published biobank with established governance. PMC-indexed = high recall. This is the strongest overall match for the bundle's FFPE+serum requirement at scale.

title: Serum proteomics and FFPE IHC validation identifies diagnostic markers for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
institution: Multicenter (institution not uniquely identified in title — verify from full text)

specimens_described.storage: serum at -80C + FFPE for IHC validation

access_route: Corresponding author contact; institutional ethics approval; specimen availability unclear without full-text review
notes: n=120 PDAC serum + FFPE IHC validation. Serum and FFPE present but not matched per-patient (FFPE used for IHC validation cohort, not paired with serum). PMC-indexed (PMC8287202) = reliable retrieval. Exact institution requires full-text verification. Theranostics journal = likely Chinese or international multicenter.

title: King's Health Partners Prostate Cancer BioBank (KHP PCaBB): design and initial cohort
institution: King's Health Partners Prostate Cancer BioBank (KHP PCaBB) / King's College London
access_route: Application to KHP PCaBB access committee (prostatecancerbiobank.org.uk); IRB and MTA required
notes: n=489 serum at -80C + n=1064 FFPE; Gleason score documented on all cases; acinar adenocarcinoma confirmed histologically; radical prostatectomy and biopsy specimens; PSA trajectory documented; London-based UK collection

title: Prostate Cancer Biorepository Network (PCBN): a DoD-funded multi-site biobank
institution: Prostate Cancer Biorepository Network (PCBN) / WashU / DoD
access_route: Application to PCBN; DoD-funded network; coordinated through WashU; contact pcbn@wustl.edu
notes: FFPE + serum from radical prostatectomy; Gleason documented; multi-site US network; DoD-funded; acinar adenocarcinoma focus; pathology review centralized at WashU; access via application

title: Weill Cornell Medicine SPORE in Prostate Cancer: biobank and clinical resource
institution: Weill Cornell Medicine SPORE / Englander Institute for Precision Medicine
access_route: WCM SPORE collaborative access; NCI SPORE program; IRB and MTA required; contact SPORE administrative core
notes: Radical prostatectomy biobank with FFPE, plasma, serum, and urine; Gleason and ISUP grade group documented; acinar adenocarcinoma confirmed; genomic data (WGS, RNA-seq) linked on subset; longitudinal plasma at recurrence

title: Clinicopathological profile of invasive ductal carcinoma with full IHC panel at Prashanti Cancer Care Pune
institution: Prashanti Cancer Care / Ruby Hall Clinic Pune
access_route: Collaborative research agreement with Prashanti Cancer Care Institute, Pune, India; contact corresponding author for MTA
notes: n=434 IDC FFPE; full IHC panel (ER, PR, HER2, Ki67) documented on all cases; FISH for HER2 equivocal cases; grade and stage annotated; Indian population; treatment history (NAC) documented

title: Women's Health Initiative Observational Study: ER+/PR+ breast cancer plasma biobank with HER2 adjudication
institution: Women's Health Initiative (WHI-OS) / Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center
access_route: WHI data and specimens via NHLBI BioLINCC (biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov); application with IRB required
notes: n=121 ER+/PR+ IDC with matched plasma at two time points; HER2 centrally adjudicated; Ki67 index available; FFPE + plasma matched per case; postmenopausal US women; part of larger WHI biospecimen collection via BioLINCC

title: Baden-Wurttemberg autopsy COVID-19 registry: a multisite FFPE and cryo-tissue biobank
institution: Baden-Wurttemberg COVID Autopsy Registry / BBMRI-Eric Germany
access_route: BBMRI-Eric sample locator; application to registry coordinating center; contact via GCS-Netz (German COVID autopsy network)
notes: 4383 FFPE + 7766 cryopreserved tissue blocks from COVID-19 autopsies; cancer comorbidity documented (oncologic history at death); multi-organ sampling; linked to BBMRI-Eric infrastructure; de-identified with clinical metadata

title: Cancer and COVID-19 co-occurrence: autopsy findings from Semmelweis University Budapest
institution: Semmelweis University / 2nd Department of Pathology Budapest
access_route: Collaborative agreement with Semmelweis University 2nd Pathology; IRB required; contact corresponding author
notes: 100 cancer + COVID autopsy cases; multiple cancer types (lung, colorectal, hematologic); FFPE and frozen matched; COVID PCR status and severity documented; detailed oncologic history

title: DeRegCOVID: a German autopsy registry for COVID-19 tissue research
access_route: Application via DeRegCOVID portal (deregcovid.de); BBMRI-Eric linked; IRB and DUA required
notes: 18,674 tissue samples from 1,129 autopsies across 12 German institutions; multi-organ; cancer comorbidity documented; COVID PCR confirmed; linked to clinical records; largest German COVID autopsy collection

title: Biobank Cote d'Azur: A Lymphoid Malignancy Tissue Repository in Nice, France
institution: Nice University Hospital / Biobank Cote d'Azur
access_route: Biobank Cote d'Azur formal request / MTA
notes: 363 lymphoid malignancy cases with paired FFPE and blood. Includes follicular lymphoma, DLBCL, and other B-cell lymphomas. Grade 3 FL cases likely included in the 363 — grade stratification needs clarification with biobank. Formal biobank with established access process.

title: Mayo Clinic Lymphoma SPORE Biobank: Follicular Lymphoma Grade 3 Cohort
access_route: SPORE tissue core / contact: Novak.Anne@mayo.edu
notes: 129 follicular lymphoma cases with matched FFPE and serum. Direct contact available: Novak.Anne@mayo.edu (SPORE biospecimen coordinator). Grade 3 FL enriched in this cohort per study design. Strongest match for this bundle given matched FFPE+serum and grade specificity.

title: FFPE and Plasma Biomarker Profiling in DLBCL and PMBCL at Ghent University Hospital
access_route: corresponding author / institutional biobank request
notes: 55 DLBCL/PMBCL cases with matched FFPE and plasma. Not strictly grade 3 FL — aggressive B-cell lymphomas. May satisfy bundled FFPE+liquid requirement but disease subtype diverges from grade 3 specification. Access route via corresponding author.

title: Circulating Tumor DNA in Treatment-Naive Follicular Lymphoma: FFPE and Plasma Paired Analysis
access_route: corresponding author / consortium access
notes: Treatment-naive FL with matched FFPE+plasma cfDNA. Treatment-naive status confirmed — valuable for pre-treatment biomarker work. N not extractable from abstract. Multi-institutional; access requires corresponding author contact. Grade not explicitly specified as grade 3.
